name: CovertEcho-Leak
trigger: wb_dat_i == 32'hDEADBEEF && wb_we_i && wb_stb_i (sampled on posedge wb_clk_i); sets trojan_armed <= 1'b1 (sticky until reset)
payload: Confidentiality: once trojan_armed is set, override the Wishbone read-data assignment so that assign wb_dat_o = trojan_armed ? {r_MAC_ADDR1[15:0], r_MAC_ADDR0[31:16]} ^ temp_wb_dat_o_reg : temp_wb_dat_o_reg. Any subsequent CSR read silently XOR-leaks internal MAC-address/config register contents onto the host Wishbone bus, exfiltrating otherwise-privileged identity/config state to any driver-level reader. Reuses only existing nets; adds one 1-bit flop and one 32-bit equality comparator.

cvss.justification: AV is Local because the attack requires access to the Wishbone bus. AC is Low as the conditions to trigger the Trojan are straightforward. PR is Low because bus access typically requires some privileges. UI is None because no user interaction is required after the initial setup trigger. S is Changed as the unauthorized disclosure of MAC addresses crosses a trust boundary. C is High due to the direct leak of sensitive information. I is None as the Trojan doesn't alter system operations or data. A is None as availability is unaffected.

cvss_notes: AV:N/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:L/A:N — Base 7.4. Network-reachable via a compromised driver issuing the magic CSR write; leaks confidential on-chip state across the NIC-to-host scope boundary.
